subject: [PATCH 2/4] mm: compaction: support non-movable compaction for pageblock requests

2026-06-26.patch_summary: This patch modifies the Linux kernel's compaction code to support non-movable compaction for pageblock requests. It relaxes the restrictions on type mixing and pollution, allowing whole-block requests to scan blocks of other types. The exception is CMA (Contiguous Memory Allocation) blocks, which remain strictly typed due to their sticky nature. The patch aims to improve the efficiency of defrag_mode by enabling non-movable direct compaction, making it more useful for producing whole blocks that can be converted wholesale on allocation.
